heading: Supplemental agricultural disaster assistance
label: 9081 Supplemental agricultural disaster assistance
notes: References in TextThe Food Security Act of 1985, referred to in subsec. (c)(3)(A)(ii), is , , . children.label: i
children.text: a beginning farmer or rancher;

children.label: ii
children.text: a socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a limited resource farmer or rancher; or

children.label: B
children.text: a veteran farmer or rancher, as defined in .section 2279(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “eligible producer on a farm” means an individual or entity described in subparagraph (B) that, as determined by the Secretary, assumes the production and market risks associated with the agricultural production of crops or livestock.

children.label: i
children.text: a citizen of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: a resident alien;

children.label: iii
children.text: an Indian tribe or tribal organization (as those terms are defined in );section 5304 of title 25

children.label: iv
children.text: a partnership of citizens of the United States; or

children.label: v
children.text: a corporation, limited liability corporation, or other farm organizational structure organized under State law.

children.heading: Farm-raised fish
children.label: 3 Farm-raised fish
children.text: The term “farm-raised fish” means any aquatic species that is propagated and reared in a controlled environment.

children.label: A
children.text: cattle (including dairy cattle);

children.label: B
children.text: bison;

children.label: C
children.text: poultry;

children.label: D
children.text: sheep;

children.label: E
children.text: swine;

children.label: F
children.text: horses; and

children.label: G
children.text: other livestock, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 5 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.label: A
children.text: attacks by animals reintroduced into the wild by the Federal Government or protected by Federal law, including wolves and avian predators;

children.label: B
children.text: adverse weather, as determined by the Secretary, during the calendar year, including losses due to hurricanes, floods, blizzards, disease, wildfires, extreme heat, and extreme cold, on the condition that in the case of the death loss of unweaned livestock due to that adverse weather, the Secretary may disregard any management practice, vaccination protocol, or lack of vaccination by the eligible producer on a farm; or

children.label: i
children.text: is caused or transmitted by a vector; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not susceptible to control by vaccination or acceptable management practices.

children.heading: Losses due to predation
children.label: A Losses due to predation
children.text: Indemnity payments to an eligible producer on a farm under paragraph (1)(A) shall be made at a rate of 100 percent of the market value of the affected livestock on the applicable date, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Losses due to adverse weather or disease
children.label: B Losses due to adverse weather or disease
children.text: Indemnity payments to an eligible producer on a farm under subparagraph (B) or (C) of paragraph (1) shall be made at a rate of 75 percent of the market value of the affected livestock on the applicable date, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Determination of market value
children.label: C Determination of market value
children.text: In determining the market value described in subparagraphs (A) and (B), the Secretary may consider the ability of eligible producers to document regional price premiums for affected livestock that exceed the national average market price for those livestock.

children.label: i
children.text: the day before the date of death of the livestock; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the day before the date of the event that caused the harm to the livestock that resulted in a reduced sale price.

children.heading: Special rule for payments made due to disease
children.label: 3 Special rule for payments made due to disease
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that payments made to an eligible producer under paragraph (1) are not made for the same livestock losses for which compensation is provided pursuant to .section 8306(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: be made if the sale occurs within a reasonable period following the event, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: be reduced by the amount that the producer received for the sale.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of unborn livestock death losses incurred on or after , the Secretary shall make an additional payment to eligible producers on farms that have incurred such losses in excess of the normal mortality due to a condition specified in paragraph (1).January 1, 2024

children.label: i
children.text: determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: less than or equal to 85 percent of the payment rate established with respect to the lowest weight class of the livestock, as determined by the Secretary, acting through the Administrator of the Farm Service Agency.

children.label: i
children.text: subparagraph (A), (B), or (F) of subsection (a)(4), by 1;

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (D) of such subsection, by 2;

children.label: iii
children.text: subparagraph (E) of such subsection, by 12; and

children.label: iv
children.text: subparagraph (G) of such subsection, by the average number of birthed animals (for one gestation cycle) for the species of each such livestock, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Unborn livestock death losses defined
children.label: D Unborn livestock death losses defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “unborn livestock death losses” means losses of any livestock described in subparagraph (A), (B), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of subsection (a)(4) that was gestating on the date of the death of the livestock.

children.label: I
children.text: owned;

children.label: II
children.text: leased;

children.label: III
children.text: purchased;

children.label: IV
children.text: entered into a contract to purchase;

children.label: V
children.text: is a contract grower; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the current production year; or

children.label: bb
children.text: subject to paragraph (3)(B)(ii), 1 or both of the 2 production years immediately preceding the current production year.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: The term “covered livestock” does not include livestock that were or would have been in a feedlot, on the beginning date of the qualifying drought or fire condition, as a part of the normal business operation of the eligible livestock producer, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Drought monitor
children.label: B Drought monitor
children.text: The term “drought monitor” means a system for classifying drought severity according to a range of abnormally dry to exceptional drought, as defined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: is an owner, cash or share lessee, or contract grower of covered livestock that provides the pastureland or grazing land, including cash-leased pastureland or grazing land, for the livestock;

children.label: II
children.text: provides the pastureland or grazing land for covered livestock, including cash-leased pastureland or grazing land that is physically located in a county affected by drought;

children.label: III
children.text: certifies grazing loss; and

children.label: IV
children.text: meets all other eligibility requirements established under this subsection.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: The term “eligible livestock producer” does not include an owner, cash or share lessee, or contract grower of livestock that rents or leases pastureland or grazing land owned by another person on a rate-of-gain basis.

children.heading: Normal carrying capacity
children.label: D Normal carrying capacity
children.text: The term “normal carrying capacity”, with respect to each type of grazing land or pastureland in a county, means the normal carrying capacity, as determined under paragraph (3)(D)(i), that would be expected from the grazing land or pastureland for livestock during the normal grazing period, in the absence of a drought or fire that diminishes the production of the grazing land or pastureland.

children.heading: Normal grazing period
children.label: E Normal grazing period
children.text: The term “normal grazing period”, with respect to a county, means the normal grazing period during the calendar year for the county, as determined under paragraph (3)(D)(i).

children.label: A
children.text: a drought condition, as described in paragraph (3); or

children.label: B
children.text: fire, as described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Assistance for losses due to drought conditions
children.label: 3 Assistance for losses due to drought conditions
I
children.text: is native or improved pastureland with permanent vegetative cover; or

children.label: II
children.text: is planted to a crop planted specifically for the purpose of providing grazing for covered livestock.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: ii Exclusions
children.text: An eligible livestock producer may not receive assistance under this subsection for grazing losses that occur on land used for haying or grazing under the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 3831

children.label: I
children.text: the monthly feed cost for all covered livestock owned or leased by the eligible livestock producer, as determined under subparagraph (C); or

children.label: II
children.text: the monthly feed cost calculated by using the normal carrying capacity of the eligible grazing land of the eligible livestock producer.

children.heading: Partial compensation
children.label: ii Partial compensation
children.text: In the case of an eligible livestock producer that sold or otherwise disposed of covered livestock due to drought conditions in 1 or both of the 2 production years immediately preceding the current production year, as determined by the Secretary, the payment rate shall be 80 percent of the payment rate otherwise calculated in accordance with clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: 30 days;

children.label: II
children.text: a payment quantity that is equal to the feed grain equivalent, as determined under clause (ii); and

children.label: III
children.text: a payment rate that is equal to the corn price per pound, as determined under clause (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of an adult beef cow, 15.7 pounds of corn per day; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any other type of weight of livestock, an amount determined by the Secretary that represents the average number of pounds of corn per day necessary to feed the livestock.

children.label: aa
children.text: the national average corn price per bushel for the 12-month period immediately preceding March 1 of the year for which the disaster assistance is calculated; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the national average corn price per bushel for the 24-month period immediately preceding that March 1; by

children.heading: Normal grazing period and drought monitor intensity
children.label: D Normal grazing period and drought monitor intensity

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine the normal carrying capacity and normal grazing period for each type of grazing land or pastureland in the county served by the applicable committee.

children.heading: Changes
children.label: II Changes
children.text: No change to the normal carrying capacity or normal grazing period established for a county under subclause (I) shall be made unless the change is requested by the appropriate State and county Farm Service Agency committees.

children.label: aa
children.text: 4 consecutive weeks during the normal grazing period for the county, as determined by the Secretary, shall be eligible to receive assistance under this paragraph in an amount equal to 1 monthly payment using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B); or

children.label: bb
children.text: 7 of the previous 8 consecutive weeks during the normal grazing period for the county, as determined by the Secretary, shall be eligible to receive assistance under this paragraph in an amount equal to 2 monthly payments using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B).

children.label: aa
children.text: in an amount equal to 3 monthly payments using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B);

children.label: bb
children.text: if the county is rated as having a D3 (extreme drought) intensity in any area of the county for at least 4 weeks during the normal grazing period for the county, or is rated as having a D4 (exceptional drought) intensity in any area of the county at any time during the normal grazing period, in an amount equal to 4 monthly payments using the monthly payment rate determined under subparagraph (B); or

children.label: cc
children.text: if the county is rated as having a D4 (exceptional drought) intensity in any area of the county for at least 4 weeks during the normal grazing period, in an amount equal to 5 monthly payments using the monthly rate determined under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Assistance for losses due to fire on public managed land
children.label: 4 Assistance for losses due to fire on public managed land
i
children.text: the grazing losses occur on rangeland that is managed by a Federal agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible livestock producer is prohibited by the Federal agency from grazing the normal permitted livestock on the managed rangeland due to a fire.

children.heading: Payment rate
children.label: B Payment rate
children.text: The payment rate for assistance under this paragraph shall be equal to 50 percent of the monthly feed cost for the total number of livestock covered by the Federal lease of the eligible livestock producer, as determined under paragraph (3)(C).

children.label: I
children.text: beginning on the date on which the Federal agency excludes the eligible livestock producer from using the managed rangeland for grazing; and

children.label: II
children.text: ending on the last day of the Federal lease of the eligible livestock producer.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: An eligible livestock producer may only receive assistance under this paragraph for losses that occur on not more than 180 days per year.

children.heading: No duplicative payments
children.label: 5 No duplicative payments
children.text: An eligible livestock producer may elect to receive assistance for grazing or pasture feed losses due to drought conditions under paragraph (3) or fire under paragraph (4), but not both for the same loss, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Emergency assistance for livestock, honey bees, and farm-raised fish
children.label: d Emergency assistance for livestock, honey bees, and farm-raised fish

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For fiscal year 2012 and each succeeding fiscal year, the Secretary shall use the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation to provide emergency relief to eligible producers of livestock, honey bees, and farm-raised fish to aid in the reduction of losses due to disease (including cattle tick fever), adverse weather, or other conditions, such as blizzards and wildfires, as determined by the Secretary, that are not covered under subsection (b) or (c).

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Funds made available under this subsection shall be used to reduce losses caused by feed or water shortages, disease, or other factors as determined by the Secretary, including inspections of cattle tick fever.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 3 Availability of funds
children.text: Any funds made available under this subsection shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Payment rate for covered producers
children.label: 4 Payment rate for covered producers
children.text: In the case of a covered producer that is eligible to receive assistance under this subsection, the Secretary shall provide reimbursement of 90 percent of the cost of losses described in paragraph (1) or (2).

children.heading: Assistance for losses due to bird depredation
children.label: 5 Assistance for losses due to bird depredation

children.heading: Definition of farm-raised fish
children.label: A Definition of farm-raised fish
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “farm-raised fish” means fish propagated and reared in a controlled fresh water environment.

children.heading: Payments
children.label: B Payments
children.text: Eligible producers of farm-raised fish, including fish grown as food for human consumption, shall be eligible to receive payments under this subsection to aid in the reduction of losses due to piscivorous birds.

children.label: I
children.text: costs associated with the deterrence of piscivorous birds;

children.label: II
children.text: the value of lost fish and revenue due to bird depredation; and

children.label: III
children.text: costs associated with disease loss from bird depredation.

children.heading: Minimum rate
children.label: ii Minimum rate
children.text: The payment rate for payments under subparagraph (B) shall be not less than $600 per acre of farm-raised fish.

children.label: i
children.text: the applicable payment rate under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: 85 percent of the total number of acres of farm-raised fish farms that the eligible producer has in production for the calendar year.

children.heading: Eligible orchardist
children.label: A Eligible orchardist
children.text: The term “eligible orchardist” means a person that produces annual crops from trees for commercial purposes.

children.heading: Natural disaster
children.label: B Natural disaster
children.text: The term “natural disaster” means plant disease, insect infestation, drought, fire, freeze, flood, earthquake, lightning, or other occurrence, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Nursery tree grower
children.label: C Nursery tree grower
children.text: The term “nursery tree grower” means a person who produces nursery, ornamental, fruit, nut, or Christmas trees for commercial sale, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Tree
children.label: D Tree
children.text: The term “tree” includes a tree, bush, and vine.

children.label: i
children.text: under paragraph (3) to eligible orchardists and nursery tree growers that planted trees for commercial purposes but lost the trees as a result of a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: under paragraph (3)(B) to eligible orchardists and nursery tree growers that have a production history for commercial purposes on planted or existing trees but lost the trees as a result of a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: An eligible orchardist or nursery tree grower shall qualify for assistance under subparagraph (A) only if the tree mortality of the eligible orchardist or nursery tree grower, as a result of damaging weather or related condition, exceeds normal mortality.

children.label: i
children.text: reimbursement of 65 percent of the cost of replanting trees lost due to a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary, in excess of normal mortality; or

children.label: ii
children.text: at the option of the Secretary, sufficient seedlings to reestablish a stand; and

children.label: B
children.text: reimbursement of 65 percent of the cost of pruning, removal, and other costs incurred by an eligible orchardist or nursery tree grower to salvage existing trees or, in the case of tree mortality, to prepare the land to replant trees as a result of damage or tree mortality due to a natural disaster, as determined by the Secretary, in excess of normal tree damage or mortality.

children.heading: Definitions of legal entity and person
children.label: A Definitions of legal entity and person
children.text: In this paragraph, the terms “legal entity” and “person” have the meaning given those terms in section 1001(a) of the Food Security Act of 1985 ().7 U.S.C. 1308(a)

children.heading: Acres
children.label: B Acres
children.text: The total quantity of acres planted to trees or tree seedlings for which a person or legal entity shall be entitled to receive payments under this subsection may not exceed 1,000 acres.

children.heading: Payment rate for beginning and veteran producers
children.label: 5 Payment rate for beginning and veteran producers
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), in the case of a beginning farmer or rancher or a veteran farmer or rancher (as those terms are defined in subsection (a) of ) that is eligible to receive assistance under this subsection, the Secretary shall provide reimbursement of 75 percent of the costs under subparagraphs (A)(i) and (B) of paragraph (3).section 2279 of this title

children.heading: Definitions of legal entity and person
children.label: 1 Definitions of legal entity and person
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “legal entity” and “person” have the meaning given those terms in section 1001(a) of the Food Security Act of 1985 ().7 U.S.C. 1308(a)

children.heading: Amount
children.label: 2 Amount
children.text: The total amount of disaster assistance payments received, directly or indirectly, by a person or legal entity (excluding a joint venture or general partnership) under subsection (c) may not exceed $125,000 for any crop year.

children.heading: Direct attribution
children.label: 3 Direct attribution
children.text: Subsections (e) and (f) of section 1001 of the Food Security Act of 1985 () or any successor provisions relating to direct attribution shall apply with respect to assistance provided under this section.7 U.S.C. 1308
